stem: A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of $13$ cm and one leg of $5$ cm. Find the length of the other leg.
A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of $10$ cm and one leg of $8$ cm. Find the length of the other leg.
A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of $17$ cm and one leg of $15$ cm. Find the length of the other leg.
A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of $25$ cm and one leg of $24$ cm. Find the length of the other leg.
A 10 m ladder leans against a wall. The foot of the ladder is 4 m from the base of the wall. Find the height the ladder reaches up the wall. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
hint: The ladder is the hypotenuse. Use $h^2 = 10^2 - 4^2$.

stem: A rectangular field is 30 m wide. A diagonal fence across the field is 34 m long. Find the length of the field.
hint: The diagonal is the hypotenuse. Use $l^2 = 34^2 - 30^2$.

stem: A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of $20$ cm and one leg of $12$ cm. Find the length of the other leg.
A ramp is 6 m long and rises 3 m vertically. Find the horizontal distance covered by the ramp. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
hint: The ramp length is the hypotenuse. Use $d^2 = 6^2 - 3^2$.

stem: An isosceles triangle has two equal sides of $13$ cm and a base of $10$ cm. Find the perpendicular height of the triangle.
hint: A perpendicular from the apex bisects the base, creating a right-angled triangle with hypotenuse $13$ cm and base leg $5$ cm.
explanation: The perpendicular bisects the base into two halves of $5$ cm. The right-angled triangle has hypotenuse $13$ cm and one leg $5$ cm. $h^2 = 13^2 - 5^2 = 169 - 25 = 144$, so $h = \sqrt{144} = 12$ cm.

stem: A square has a diagonal of $10$ cm. Find the side length of the square. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
hint: Both legs of the right-angled triangle formed by the diagonal are equal. Use $s^2 + s^2 = 10^2$.

stem: A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of $26$ cm and one leg of $10$ cm. Find the area of the triangle in $\text{cm}^2$.
hint: First find the other leg using Pythagoras' theorem, then use $A = \frac{1}{2} \times base \times height$.

stem: A rhombus has a side length of $13$ cm. One of its diagonals is $24$ cm long. Find the length of the other diagonal. (The diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angles.)
hint: Each diagonal is halved at the centre. Use the half-lengths to form a right-angled triangle with the side as the hypotenuse.
explanation: Half the known diagonal $= 12$ cm. The other half-diagonal $d$ satisfies $d^2 = 13^2 - 12^2 = 169 - 144 = 25$, so $d = 5$ cm. The full diagonal $= 2 \times 5 = 10$ cm.
